slices.focus: Work stress and health boundary reset
slices.summary: A Gulf tropical storm watch compounded with callback crunch triggered severe reflux and chest-tightness that woke him at night. Supervisor Sharon intervened to implement a shared callback board after seeing the strain. Committed to no evening resident calls, daily board use, and a morning anti-anxiety script on the kitchen counter to protect sleep and health.
open_loops: Track two weeks of sleep and symptom data to verify boundaries actually improve health outcomes
If chest-tightness persists or changes character, commit to seeking medical attention rather than just process fixes
Clearly communicate the no-evening-calls policy change to the team
decisions: Stop taking resident calls after dinner except for genuine deadline emergencies
Use the shared callback board daily to prevent hidden backlog from accumulating mentally or physically
Place a brief anti-anxiety script on the kitchen counter for early mornings
